About This Story

In this inspiring story, Layla faces many challenges. She starts with a good life but loses everything. She feels alone and drifts from place to place. One wrong turn leads her to a soccer game. There, she finds hope. Layla builds a school for refugee children. This story shows how one person can change a neighborhood and help many others. It is about belonging, community, and hope.

Imagine being forced to leave your family forever. Imagine feeling alone every day. Layla had everything, then she lost it all. Her family disowned her. She drifted from city to city, working small jobs. She felt lost and sad. During Ramadan, she broke her fast alone. Then one wrong turn changed everything. She saw children playing soccer with a deflated ball. They were barefoot and refugees. In that moment, Layla found her purpose. This is the story of how one wrong turn led to a school that changed many lives.

Many refugees arrive in new countries with little support. They face language barriers and educational challenges. Some children cannot read or write. They are placed in wrong grade levels. Schools often fail to help them succeed. This story shows how one person created a school specifically for refugee children.
